meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Mastering User Onboarding to Finish Projects
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn how to implement strategic user onboarding to drive retention and completion. A practical guide using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of user feedback loops. By implementing a high-intent onboarding flow, you validate your core value proposition immediately. This forces feature prioritization, keeps you motivated through user success, and transforms your 'pet project' into a scalable, production-ready SaaS application.
content.intro:
The graveyard of abandoned GitHub repositories is littered with projects that lacked a single, critical component: a reason for the user to stay. You know the feeling—you spend weeks on the backend, only to lose momentum because the app feels like a hollow shell. The excitement fades, the complexity mounts, and the project becomes another 'in-progress' casualty on your resume.

But what if you could change the game? By focusing on user onboarding first, you stop building for yourself and start building for an audience. This isn't just about UI; it's about engineering the 'Aha!' moment that turns a visitor into a user, providing the validation you need to push through the finish line.

steps.title: Define the 'Aha!' Moment
steps.description: Identify the single feature that provides the most value. If a user doesn't reach this within 60 seconds, your project is failing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and identify the core value proposition. Suggest a 3-step onboarding flow that leads the user directly to the primary feature.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Identify the core value prop and map a 3-step user onboarding flow'

steps.title: Scaffold the Onboarding State
steps.description: Create a lightweight state machine to track user progress through the onboarding flow without polluting your main database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React context provider to manage onboarding state (step index, completion status, user data capture).
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a React OnboardingProvider to track user progress through steps'

steps.title: Implement Intelligent Tooltips
steps.description: Use non-intrusive UI cues to guide users. Avoid overwhelming them with modals; focus on contextual assistance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable Tooltip component that attaches to specific DOM elements based on the current onboarding step index.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Build a reusable Tooltip component for onboarding guidance'

steps.title: Data-Driven User Persistence
steps.description: Save user progress to local storage so they don't lose their place if they refresh the page, increasing completion rates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a hook that persists the onboarding state to localStorage and syncs with the database once the flow is complete.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement useOnboardingPersistence hook for localStorage syncing'

steps.title: Validate and Iterate
steps.description: Add a simple analytics event to track where users drop off in your onboarding flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add a tracking function to the onboarding flow that logs the current step to a mock analytics service.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add analytics logging to onboarding completion steps'

pro_tips: Never build a landing page before you build the onboarding flow; if the flow is boring, the landing page is a lie.
Use 'Progressive Disclosure': Show only the information necessary for the immediate task, hiding complex settings until the user is invested.
Implement a 'Skip' button. If users can't skip your onboarding, you aren't building a tool, you're building a prison.
Always assume the user is distracted. If they leave and come back, your onboarding should detect their state and resume from the last step.
Focus on 'Time-to-Value' (TTV) as your primary metric; the shorter the TTV, the higher the chance you will actually finish the app.
content.next_steps: Deploy your onboarding flow to a staging environment, share it with three peers for feedback, and document the conversion rate in your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your best ideas rot in a private repo. By mastering user-centric development, you gain the skills that companies actually pay for. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Create a lightweight state machine to track user progress through the onboarding flow without polluting your main database.
Use non-intrusive UI cues to guide users. Avoid overwhelming them with modals; focus on contextual assistance.
Save user progress to local storage so they don't lose their place if they refresh the page, increasing completion rates.
Add a simple analytics event to track where users drop off in your onboarding flow.
